perturbation.original_text: Each party represents and warrants that it has the right to disclose any information provided to the other party in furtherance of the purpose described in Section 1, without violating any agreement with or right of any other person or company. Confidential Information disclosed by a party hereunder may include Confidential Information of a third party, provided that the third party has authorized such disclosure, and in such event this Agreement shall apply equally to such Confidential Information and shall inure to the benefit of such third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party represents and warrants that it has the right to disclose any information provided to the other party in furtherance of the purpose described in Section 1, without violating any agreement with or right of any other person or company. This agreement shall r emain in effect for a period of Three (3) Years or the life of any Agreements, Contracts or Commercial Arrangements which may arise from the joint efforts of the parties hereto, whichever shall be longer.
perturbation.explanation: By moving the term clause into the 'Right to Disclose' section, it is not readily apparent that the confidentiality obligations extend for a specific duration, potentially misleading a party into believing the obligations are perpetual even if the agreement has a stated term. The term of the agreement belongs in a 'Term' or 'Duration' section.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Limitation Act, 2002, S.O. 2002, c. 24, Sch. B
perturbation.law_explanation: Moving the term clause to the Right to Disclose section obscures its relevance, making it difficult to determine the applicable limitation period for claims related to the NDA, potentially conflicting with the Ontario's Limitation Act, 2002. Section 4 of the Act requires that claims should be issued within a specific period of time.

perturbation.justification: The original contract clearly outlines confidentiality obligations, while the changed text places the term clause within the 'Right to Disclose' section, obscuring the duration of these obligations. This could mislead a party into believing confidentiality is perpetual. The Law Explanation supports this by stating that this obscures the limitation period for claims related to the NDA, potentially conflicting with Ontario's Limitation Act, 2002. Scraped Law Snippet 1 references the Limitations Act, 2002, and its various amendments, which implies claims should be issued within a specific period. By obscuring the duration of the confidentiality obligations, the changed text weakens the ability to determine the applicable limitation period, leading to a potential legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Party One and P a r t y T w o each acknowledge that compliance with the provisions of this Agreement is necessary to protec t their proprietary interes ts. Each party further acknowledges that any unauthorized use or disclosure to any person or entity in breach of this Agreement will result in irreparable and continuing damage, and that each party shall be authorized and entitled to obtain immediate injun ctive relief and any other rights or remedies to which it may be entitled. If either party violates any of the above agreements, it acknowledges that money damages will be an inadequate remedy and that the violated party will be entitled to specific perfo rmance or to injunctive relief to prohibit the violating party from continuing to violate this agreement even if no money damages can be proven.
perturbation.changed_text: Party One and P a r t y T w o each acknowledge that compliance with the provisions of this Agreement is necessary to protec t their proprietary interes ts. Each party further acknowledges that any unauthorized use or disclosure to any person or entity in breach of this Agreement will result in irreparable and continuing damage, and that each party shall be authorized and entitled to obtain immediate injun ctive relief and any other rights or remedies to which it may be entitled. Confidential Information shall include, but not be limited to, all materials marked "Confidential Information".
perturbation.explanation: Moving the definition of 'Confidential Information' from Section 2 to the remedies section undermines its primary function of clearly defining the scope of protected information covered by the NDA. This obscures the specific information protected and could hinder the ability to obtain remedies for its breach.
perturbation.law_explanation: Moving the definition of 'Confidential Information' to the remedies section undermines clarity and definiteness, potentially conflicting with Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 33. This section requires reasonable certainty in contract terms, and placing a fundamental definition in an unusual location diminishes this certainty.

perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines the repercussions of violating the agreement, including the availability of injunctive relief. The changed text introduces an element (definition of Confidential Information) into the remedies section that weakens the original intent by obfuscating what constitutes 'Confidential Information.' The Law Explanation states the change undermines clarity and definiteness, potentially conflicting with Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 33. This section requires reasonable certainty in contract terms, and placing a fundamental definition in an unusual location diminishes this certainty, thus creating a structural flaw that leads to a legal contradiction as the enforceability of the clause is affected. The second snippet does not provide legal information that contradicts the previous analysis or that affects this decision.
